hook.text: A scout watches a player and says 'he's got it.' A model analyzes 10,000 data points and disagrees.

buildup.text: Statistical scouting models predict future performance from college stats, combine results, and tracking data.

discovery.text: The best scouting combines both: data identifies candidates, eyes evaluate intangibles like leadership and drive.

twist.text: Tom Brady was drafted 199th overall. No model predicted his greatness. Outliers humble every system.

climax.text: Data reduces mistakes in scouting. It doesn't eliminate them. The human element remains irreplaceable.

punchline.text: Data narrows the search. Eyes make the final call.

quiz.question: What is the best approach to scouting athletes?
options: Combining statistical models to identify candidates with human evaluation of intangible qualities
Relying only on data and ignoring scouting
Relying only on traditional scouting and ignoring data
